answer:
**1. In-Depth User Understanding:** 
Understanding the believers' profound need for pure and uncorrupted guidance, the scriptures establish that the Prophet's speech is never driven by personal desire. Surah An-Najm 53:3-4 directly addresses this by confirming that he does not speak from his own inclination, and that his words are nothing but a divine revelation revealed to him by God [1-3].

**2. Robust and Enduring Framework:** 
The binding authority of the Prophet's teachings provides a resilient foundation for the Islamic way of life, built upon the unwavering command to obey his instructions completely. Surah Al-Hashr 59:7 constructs this steadfast rule by commanding believers to take whatever the Messenger gives them and to refrain from whatever he forbids [4]. Furthermore, Surah An-Nisa 4:80 reinforces this enduring theological framework by explicitly equating obedience to the Messenger with obedience to God Himself [5].

**3. Elegant and Lucid Presentation:** 
To maintain the beauty, clarity, and grace of this divine instruction, the exact words of the Prophet must be preserved and presented without alteration. The Prophet elegantly emphasized this precise preservation when teaching a companion a specific supplication to recite before sleep; when the companion mistakenly substituted the word "Apostle" for "Prophet," the Prophet immediately corrected him, instructing him to use the exact phrase: "Your Prophet whom You have sent" [6]. 

**4. Seamless and Accessible Experience:** 
To ensure the religion remains easily accessible and uncorrupted for all generations, there is a severe warning against fabricating or altering the Prophet's teachings. The Prophet established a clear and simple boundary to protect the transmission of his words, stating that whoever tells a lie against him intentionally will surely take his place in the Hellfire [7, 8]. This strict preservation guarantees that the light of his guidance is transmitted with seamless accuracy from those who were present to those who were absent [9, 10].

bikitabika-l-ladhi anzalta wa bina-biyika-l ladhi arsalta" (O Allah! I surrender to You and entrust all my affairs to You and depend upon You for Your Blessings both with hope and fear of You. There is no fleeing from You, and there is no place of protection and safety except with You O Allah! I be-lieve in Your Book (the Qur'an) which You have revealed and in Your Prophet (Muhammad) whom You have sent). Then if you die on that very night, you will die with faith (i.e. or the religion of Islam). Let the aforesaid words be your last utterance (before sleep)." I repeated it before the Prophet and when I reached "Allahumma amantu bikitabika-l-ladhi anzalta (O Allah I believe in Your Book which You have revealed)." I said, "Wa-rasulika (and your Apostle)." The Prophet said, "No, (but say): 'Wanabiyika-l-ladhi arsalta (Your Prophet whom You have sent), instead."

“They have certainly disbelieved who say, "God is the Messiah, the son of Mary" while the Messiah has said, "O Children of Israel, worship God, my Lord and your Lord." Indeed, he who associates others with God - God has forbidden him Paradise, and his refuge is the Fire. And there are not for the wrongdoers any helpers.”

6. And what God restored [of property] to His Messenger from them - you did not spur for it [in an expedition] any horses or camels,1540 but God gives His messengers power over whom He wills, and God is over all things competent.
